description: Initial execution result for task task3

data.task_objective: Build the Interview Orchestration Engine: manage dynamic question flows, call AI services to generate technical and behavioral questions (including follow-ups), and track session state.
data.context:
Task task0:
  Objective: Define the database schema for job descriptions, candidate resumes, interview sessions, AI-generated questions, candidate responses, evaluation scores, email logs, and final reports.
  Result: ```json
{
  "schemas": {
    "job_descriptions": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "job_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Unique identifier for the job description",
          "primaryKey": true
        },
        "title": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Job title"
        },
        "description": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Full job description text"
        },
        "requirements": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Job requirements"
        },
        "responsibilities": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Job responsibilities"
        },
        "department": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Department the job belongs to"
        },
        "location": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Job location"
        },
        "date_posted": {
          "type": "string",
          "format": "date",
          "description": "Date the job was posted"
        },
        "embedding": {
          "type": "array",
          "items": {
            "type": "number"
          },
          "description": "Vector embedding of the job description"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "job_id",
        "title",
        "description"
      ]
    },
    "candidate_resumes": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "candidate_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Unique identifier for the candidate",
          "primaryKey": true
        },
        "name": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Candidate's full name"
        },
        "email": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Candidate's email address"
        },
        "phone": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Candidate's phone number"
        },
        "resume_text": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Full text of the candidate's resume"
        },
        "skills": {
          "type": "array",
          "items": {
            "type": "string"
          },
          "description": "List of skills extracted from the resume"
        },
        "experience": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Candidate's work experience"
        },
        "education": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Candidate's education details"
        },
        "embedding": {
          "type": "array",
          "items": {
            "type": "number"
          },
          "description": "Vector embedding of the resume"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "candidate_id",
        "name",
        "email",
        "resume_text"
      ]
    },
    "interview_sessions": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "session_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Unique identifier for the interview session",
          "primaryKey": true
        },
        "candidate_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing candidate_resumes"
        },
        "job_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing job_descriptions"
        },
        "start_time": {
          "type": "string",
          "format": "date-time",
          "description": "Timestamp of when the interview started"
        },
        "end_time": {
          "type": "string",
          "format": "date-time",
          "description": "Timestamp of when the interview ended"
        },
        "status": {
          "type": "string",
          "enum": [
            "scheduled",
            "in_progress",
            "completed",
            "cancelled"
          ],
          "description": "Status of the interview session"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "session_id",
        "candidate_id",
        "job_id",
        "start_time"
      ]
    },
    "ai_generated_questions": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "question_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Unique identifier for the question",
          "primaryKey": true
        },
        "session_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing interview_sessions"
        },
        "question_text": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "The text of the question generated by AI"
        },
        "question_type": {
          "type": "string",
          "enum": [
            "technical",
            "behavioral",
            "follow_up"
          ],
          "description": "Type of question"
        },
        "expected_answer": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "The expected answer or keywords for the question"
        },
         "topic": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Topic of the question"
        },
        "difficulty": {
          "type": "string",
          "enum": ["easy", "medium", "hard"],
          "description": "Difficulty level of the question"
         }
      },
      "required": [
        "question_id",
        "session_id",
        "question_text",
        "question_type"
      ]
    },
    "candidate_responses": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "response_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Unique identifier for the response",
          "primaryKey": true
        },
        "session_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing interview_sessions"
        },
        "question_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing ai_generated_questions"
        },
        "response_text": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "The candidate's answer to the question"
        },
        "timestamp": {
          "type": "string",
          "format": "date-time",
          "description": "Timestamp of when the response was recorded"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "response_id",
        "session_id",
        "question_id",
        "response_text"
      ]
    },
    "evaluation_scores": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "score_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Unique identifier for the score",
          "primaryKey": true
        },
        "session_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing interview_sessions"
        },
         "question_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing ai_generated_questions"
        },
        "category": {
          "type": "string",
          "enum": [
            "relevance",
            "depth",
            "confidence",
            "technical_accuracy",
            "communication_skills",
            "problem_solving"
          ],
          "description": "Category being evaluated"
        },
        "score": {
          "type": "number",
          "description": "Score for the category (e.g., 1-5 scale)"
        },
        "justification": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Explanation or justification for the score"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "score_id",
        "session_id",
        "category",
        "score"
      ]
    },
    "email_logs": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "email_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Unique identifier for the email",
          "primaryKey": true
        },
        "candidate_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing candidate_resumes"
        },
        "email_type": {
          "type": "string",
          "enum": [
            "invitation",
            "follow_up",
            "rejection",
            "offer"
          ],
          "description": "Type of email"
        },
        "subject": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Email subject"
        },
        "body": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Email body text"
        },
        "timestamp": {
          "type": "string",
          "format": "date-time",
          "description": "Timestamp of when the email was sent"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "email_id",
        "candidate_id",
        "email_type",
        "subject",
        "body",
        "timestamp"
      ]
    },
    "final_reports": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "report_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Unique identifier for the final report",
          "primaryKey": true
        },
        "candidate_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing candidate_resumes"
        },
        "job_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing job_descriptions"
        },
        "session_id": {
          "type": "integer",
          "description": "Foreign key referencing interview_sessions"
        },
        "resume_job_match_analysis": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Analysis of how well the resume matches the job description"
        },
        "interview_summary": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Summary of the interview session"
        },
        "technical_assessment": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Assessment of the candidate's technical skills"
        },
        "behavioral_assessment": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Assessment of the candidate's behavioral traits"
        },
        "strengths": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "List of the candidate's strengths"
        },
        "weaknesses": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "List of the candidate's weaknesses"
        },
        "final_recommendation": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Final recommendation (e.g., hire, no hire, further review)"
        },
        "overall_score": {
          "type": "number",
          "description": "Overall score for the candidate"
        },
        "scoring_breakdown": {
          "type": "string",
          "description": "Detailed breakdown of the scoring across different categories (JSON format)"
        },
         "report_generation_date": {
          "type": "string",
          "format": "date-time",
          "description": "Timestamp of when the report was generated"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "report_id",
        "candidate_id",
        "job_id",
        "session_id"
      ]
    }
  }
}
```
Task task1:
  Objective: Design the RESTful API specification (endpoints, request/response models) for core operations: ingesting jobs/resumes, orchestrating interviews, submitting responses, scoring, emailing, and retrieving reports.
  Result: ```yaml
openapi: 3.0.0
info:
  title: AI-Driven Interview and Candidate Evaluation System API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: RESTful API for managing jobs, resumes, interviews, evaluations, and reports.

paths:
  /jobs:
    post:
      summary: Ingest a new job description
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                job_title:
                  type: string
                  description: Title of the job
                job_description:
                  type: string
                  description: Detailed description of the job
                department:
                  type: string
                  description: Department for the job
                location:
                  type: string
                  description: Job location
                skills:
                  type: array
                  items:
                    type: string
                  description: List of required skills
              example:
                job_title: Software Engineer
                job_description: Develop and maintain software applications.  Experience with Python and Javascript required.
                department: Engineering
                location: New York, NY
                skills: [Python, Javascript, React, AWS]
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Job created successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  job_id:
                    type: string
                    description: Unique identifier for the job
                example:
                  job_id: job123
        '400':
          description: Invalid request

    get:
      summary: Retrieve a list of all jobs
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of jobs retrieved successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  type: object
                  properties:
                    job_id:
                      type: string
                      description: Unique identifier for the job
                    job_title:
                      type: string
                      description: Title of the job
                    job_description:
                      type: string
                      description: Detailed description of the job
                    department:
                      type: string
                      description: Department for the job
                    location:
                      type: string
                      description: Job location
                    skills:
                      type: array
                      items:
                        type: string
                      description: List of required skills
                  example:
                    job_id: job123
                    job_title: Software Engineer
                    job_description: Develop and maintain software applications.  Experience with Python and Javascript required.
                    department: Engineering
                    location: New York, NY
                    skills: [Python, Javascript, React, AWS]

  /jobs/{job_id}:
    get:
      summary: Retrieve a specific job by ID
      parameters:
        - in: path
          name: job_id
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
          description: Unique identifier for the job
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Job retrieved successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  job_id:
                    type: string
                    description: Unique identifier for the job
                  job_title:
                    type: string
                    description: Title of the job
                  job_description:
                    type: string
                    description: Detailed description of the job
                  department:
                    type: string
                    description: Department for the job
                  location:
                    type: string
                    description: Job location
                  skills:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      type: string
                    description: List of required skills
                example:
                  job_id: job123
                  job_title: Software Engineer
                  job_description: Develop and maintain software applications.  Experience with Python and Javascript required.
                  department: Engineering
                  location: New York, NY
                  skills: [Python, Javascript, React, AWS]
        '404':
          description: Job not found

  /resumes:
    post:
      summary: Ingest a new candidate resume
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                candidate_name:
                  type: string
                  description: Name of the candidate
                resume_text:
                  type: string
                  description: Text content of the resume
                email:
                  type: string
                  format: email
                  description: Candidate's email address
                skills:
                  type: array
                  items:
                    type: string
                  description: List of skills extracted from the resume
              example:
                candidate_name: John Doe
                resume_text: Experienced software engineer with Python and Javascript skills.
                email: john.doe@example.com
                skills: [Python, Javascript, SQL]
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Resume created successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  resume_id:
                    type: string
                    description: Unique identifier for the resume
                example:
                  resume_id: resume456
        '400':
          description: Invalid request
    get:
      summary: Retrieve a list of all resumes
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of resumes retrieved successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  type: object
                  properties:
                    resume_id:
                      type: string
                      description: Unique identifier for the resume
                    candidate_name:
                      type: string
                      description: Name of the candidate
                    resume_text:
                      type: string
                      description: Text content of the resume
                    email:
                      type: string
                      format: email
                      description: Candidate's email address
                    skills:
                      type: array
                      items:
                        type: string
                      description: List of skills extracted from the resume
                  example:
                    resume_id: resume456
                    candidate_name: John Doe
                    resume_text: Experienced software engineer with Python and Javascript skills.
                    email: john.doe@example.com
                    skills: [Python, Javascript, SQL]

  /resumes/{resume_id}:
    get:
      summary: Retrieve a specific resume by ID
      parameters:
        - in: path
          name: resume_id
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
          description: Unique identifier for the resume
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Resume retrieved successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  resume_id:
                    type: string
                    description: Unique identifier for the resume
                  candidate_name:
                    type: string
                    description: Name of the candidate
                  resume_text:
                    type: string
                    description: Text content of the resume
                  email:
                    type: string
                    format: email
                    description: Candidate's email address
                  skills:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      type: string
                    description: List of skills extracted from the resume
                example:
                  resume_id: resume456
                  candidate_name: John Doe
                  resume_text: Experienced software engineer with Python and Javascript skills.
                  email: john.doe@example.com
                  skills: [Python, Javascript, SQL]
        '404':
          description: Resume not found

  /interviews:
    post:
      summary: Start a new interview session
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                job_id:
                  type: string
                  description: ID of the job for the interview
                resume_id:
                  type: string
                  description: ID of the candidate's resume
              example:
                job_id: job123
                resume_id: resume456
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Interview session started successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  interview_id:
                    type: string
                    description: Unique identifier for the interview session
                  first_question:
                    type: string
                    description: The first question to ask the candidate
                example:
                  interview_id: interview789
                  first_question: "Tell me about your experience with Python."
        '400':
          description: Invalid request
        '404':
          description: Job or Resume not found

  /interviews/{interview_id}/questions:
    get:
      summary: Get the current or next question in the interview
      parameters:
        - in: path
          name: interview_id
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
          description: Unique identifier for the interview session
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Question retrieved successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  question:
                    type: string
                    description: The question to ask the candidate
                example:
                  question: "Explain the difference between '==' and '===' in Javascript."
        '404':
          description: Interview not found

  /interviews/{interview_id}/responses:
    post:
      summary: Submit a candidate's response to a question
      parameters:
        - in: path
          name: interview_id
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
          description: Unique identifier for the interview session
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                question:
                  type: string
                  description: The question being answered.
                response:
                  type: string
                  description: The candidate's answer to the question
              example:
                question: "Explain the difference between '==' and '===' in Javascript."
                response: "'==' compares values, while '===' compares values and types."
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Response submitted successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  next_question:
                    type: string
                    nullable: true
                    description: The next question to ask, or null if the interview is complete
                  evaluation:
                    type: object
                    description: Evaluation of the response
                    properties:
                      relevance:
                        type: number
                        format: float
                        description: Relevance score (0-1)
                      depth:
                        type: number
                        format: float
                        description: Depth score (0-1)
                      confidence:
                        type: number
                        format: float
                        description: Confidence score (0-1)
                      technical_accuracy:
                        type: number
                        format: float
                        description: Technical accuracy score (0-1)
                    example:
                      relevance: 0.9
                      depth: 0.8
                      confidence: 0.7
                      technical_accuracy: 0.95
                example:
                  next_question: "How would you optimize a slow SQL query?"
                  evaluation:
                    relevance: 0.9
                    depth: 0.8
                    confidence: 0.7
                    technical_accuracy: 0.95
        '400':
          description: Invalid request
        '404':
          description: Interview not found

  /interviews/{interview_id}/scores:
    get:
      summary: Retrieve the candidate's scores for the interview
      parameters:
        - in: path
          name: interview_id
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
          description: Unique identifier for the interview session
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Scores retrieved successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  technical_score:
                    type: number
                    format: float
                    description: Overall technical score
                  behavioral_score:
                    type: number
                    format: float
                    description: Overall behavioral score
                  overall_score:
                    type: number
                    format: float
                    description: Overall score
                  category_scores:
                    type: object
                    description: Scores for individual categories
                    properties:
                      problem_solving:
                        type: number
                        format: float
                        description: Problem-solving score
                      communication:
                        type: number
                        format: float
                        description: Communication score
                    example:
                      problem_solving: 0.85
                      communication: 0.9
                example:
                  technical_score: 0.8
                  behavioral_score: 0.9
                  overall_score: 0.85
                  category_scores:
                    problem_solving: 0.85
                    communication: 0.9
        '404':
          description: Interview not found

  /emails/invite:
    post:
      summary: Generate and send an interview invitation email
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                candidate_email:
                  type: string
                  format: email
                  description: Candidate's email address
                job_title:
                  type: string
                  description: Job title
                interview_date:
                  type: string
                  format: date-time
                  description: Date and time of the interview
                interviewer_name:
                  type: string
                  description: Name of the interviewer
              example:
                candidate_email: john.doe@example.com
                job_title: Software Engineer
                interview_date: "2024-01-20T10:00:00Z"
                interviewer_name: Jane Smith
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Email sent successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  message:
                    type: string
                    description: Confirmation message
                example:
                  message: "Interview invitation email sent to john.doe@example.com"
        '400':
          description: Invalid request
        '500':
          description: Email sending failed

  /emails/followup:
    post:
      summary: Generate and send a follow-up email to the candidate
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                candidate_email:
                  type: string
                  format: email
                  description: Candidate's email address
                interview_id:
                  type: string
                  description: Interview ID
              example:
                candidate_email: john.doe@example.com
                interview_id: interview789
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Email sent successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  message:
                    type: string
                    description: Confirmation message
                example:
                  message: "Follow-up email sent to john.doe@example.com"
        '400':
          description: Invalid request
        '500':
          description: Email sending failed

  /reports/{interview_id}:
    get:
      summary: Retrieve the final candidate evaluation report
      parameters:
        - in: path
          name: interview_id
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
          description: Unique identifier for the interview session
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Report retrieved successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  resume_job_match_analysis:
                    type: string
                    description: Analysis of how well the resume matches the job description
                  interview_summary:
                    type: string
                    description: Summary of the interview
                  technical_assessment:
                    type: string
                    description: Assessment of the candidate's technical skills
                  behavioral_assessment:
                    type: string
                    description: Assessment of the candidate's behavioral traits
                  strengths:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      type: string
                    description: List of the candidate's strengths
                  weaknesses:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      type: string
                    description: List of the candidate's weaknesses
                  final_recommendation:
                    type: string
                    description: Final recommendation (Hire/No Hire/Maybe)
                  scoring_breakdown:
                    type: object
                    description: Detailed breakdown of the candidate's scores
                    properties:
                      technical_score:
                        type: number
                        format: float
                        description: Overall technical score
                      behavioral_score:
                        type: number
                        format: float
                        description: Overall behavioral score
                      overall_score:
                        type: number
                        format: float
                        description: Overall score
                    example:
                      technical_score: 0.8
                      behavioral_score: 0.9
                      overall_score: 0.85
                example:
                  resume_job_match_analysis: "The candidate's resume closely matches the job description, with strong skills in Python and Javascript."
                  interview_summary: "The candidate performed well in the interview, demonstrating strong technical knowledge and communication skills."
                  technical_assessment: "The candidate has a solid understanding of software engineering principles and is proficient in Python and Javascript."
                  behavioral_assessment: "The candidate is a good communicator and works well in a team environment."
                  strengths: ["Technical Skills", "Communication", "Problem Solving"]
                  weaknesses: ["Lack of experience with AWS"]
                  final_recommendation: "Hire"
                  scoring_breakdown:
                    technical_score: 0.8
                    behavioral_score: 0.9

Task task2:
  Objective: Implement the Resume–Job Matching Module: parse and embed job descriptions and resumes, compute semantic similarity scores, and expose matching interface.
  Result: ```python
import nltk
import numpy as np
from sklearn.metrics.pairwise import cosine_similarity
from nltk.corpus import stopwords
from nltk.tokenize import word_tokenize
import re
from typing import List, Tuple

# Download required NLTK data (if not already downloaded)
try:
    nltk.data.find('corpora/stopwords')
except LookupError:
    nltk.download('stopwords')

try:
    nltk.data.find('tokenizers/punkt')
except LookupError:
    nltk.download('punkt')


class ResumeJobMatcher:
    """
    A class for matching resumes to job descriptions based on semantic similarity.
    """

    def __init__(self, embedding_model=None):
        """
        Initializes the ResumeJobMatcher with an optional embedding model.

        Args:
            embedding_model:  A pre-trained word embedding model (e.g., Word2Vec, GloVe).
                              If None, a simple TF-IDF based approach is used.  Must have a 'encode' method.
        """
        self.embedding_model = embedding_model
        self.stop_words = set(stopwords.words('english'))

    def preprocess_text(self, text: str) -> str:
        """
        Preprocesses the input text by removing special characters,
        converting to lowercase, and removing stop words.

        Args:
            text (str): The input text to preprocess.

        Returns:
            str: The preprocessed text.
        """
        text = re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', '', text)
        text = text.lower()
        tokens = word_tokenize(text)
        tokens = [w for w in tokens if not w in self.stop_words]
        return " ".join(tokens)

    def compute_similarity(self, embedding1: np.ndarray, embedding2: np.ndarray) -> float:
        """
        Computes the cosine similarity between two embeddings.

        Args:
            embedding1 (np.ndarray): The first embedding.
            embedding2 (np.ndarray): The second embedding.

        Returns:
            float: The cosine similarity score.
        """
        if np.linalg.norm(embedding1) == 0 or np.linalg.norm(embedding2) == 0:
            return 0.0  # Handle zero vector case
        return cosine_similarity(embedding1.reshape(1, -1), embedding2.reshape(1, -1))[0][0]

    def match(self, job_description: str, resume_text: str) -> float:
        """
        Matches a job description to a resume based on semantic similarity
        of their embeddings.

        Args:
            job_description (str): The job description text.
            resume_text (str): The resume text.

        Returns:
            float: The similarity score between the job description and resume.
        """
        preprocessed_job_description = self.preprocess_text(job_description)
        preprocessed_resume_text = self.preprocess_text(resume_text)

        if self.embedding_model:
            job_embedding = self.embedding_model.encode(preprocessed_job_description)
            resume_embedding = self.embedding_model.encode(preprocessed_resume_text)
        else:
            raise ValueError("Embedding model not provided")


        similarity_score = self.compute_similarity(job_embedding, resume_embedding)
        return similarity_score

    def extract_skills(self, text: str) -> List[str]:
        """
        Extracts skills from the given text (resume or job description).  This is a placeholder;
        a real implementation would use NLP techniques or a skills database.

        Args:
            text (str): The text to extract skills from.

        Returns:
            List[str]: A list of skills found in the text.
        """
        # Placeholder implementation: split the text into words and return unique words
        # In a real implementation, use NLP techniques to identify skills
        words = self.preprocess_text(text).split()
        return list(set(words))


    def analyze_match(self, job_description: str, resume_text: str) -> Tuple[float, List[str], List[str]]:
        """
        Analyzes the match between a job description and a resume, returning the similarity score,
        skills found in the job description, and skills found in the resume.

        Args:
            job_description (str): The job description text.
            resume_text (str): The resume text.

        Returns:
            Tuple[float, List[str], List[str]]: A tuple containing the similarity score,
                                                 list of job description skills, and list of resume skills.
        """
        similarity_score = self.match(job_description, resume_text)
        job_skills = self.extract_skills(job_description)
        resume_skills = self.extract_skills(resume_text)
        return similarity_score, job_skills, resume_skills


if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Example Usage (requires a dummy embedding model for testing)
    class DummyEmbeddingModel:
        def encode(self, text):
            # Replace with actual embedding logic in production
            return np.random.rand(100)  # Return a random vector as a placeholder

    job_description = "Software Engineer with experience in Python and Javascript"
    resume_text = "Experienced software engineer proficient in Python, Javascript, and SQL"

    embedding_model = DummyEmbeddingModel()
    matcher = ResumeJobMatcher(embedding_model=embedding_model)

    similarity_score, job_skills, resume_skills = matcher.analyze_match(job_description, resume_text)

    print(f"Similarity Score: {similarity_score}")
    print(f"Job Description Skills: {job_skills}")
    print(f"Resume Skills: {resume_skills}")

```python
import json
import logging
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple
import random

# Configure logging
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format='%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s')


class InterviewOrchestrator:
    """
    Orchestrates the interview process, managing question generation,
    tracking session state, and handling AI service calls.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        job_id: int,
        candidate_id: int,
        session_id: int,
        question_generator,  # Dependency Injection for AI question generation
        evaluation_service,  # Dependency Injection for evaluation
        database_client,  # Dependency Injection for database interaction
        initial_question_count: int = 3,
        technical_question_ratio: float = 0.6,
    ):
        """
        Initializes the InterviewOrchestrator.

        Args:
            job_id (int): The ID of the job being interviewed for.
            candidate_id (int): The ID of the candidate being interviewed.
            session_id (int): The ID of the interview session.
            question_generator: An AI service for generating interview questions. It should have a 'generate_question' method.
            evaluation_service: An AI service for evaluating candidate responses. It should have an 'evaluate_response' method.
            database_client: Client for interacting with the database.  It should have methods like 'get_job_details', 'get_candidate_details', 'store_question', 'store_response'.
            initial_question_count (int): The number of initial questions to generate (default: 3).
            technical_question_ratio (float): The ratio of technical questions to behavioral questions (default: 0.6).
        """
        self.job_id = job_id
        self.candidate_id = candidate_id
        self.session_id = session_id
        self.question_generator = question_generator
        self.evaluation_service = evaluation_service
        self.database_client = database_client
        self.question_history: List[int] = []  # List of question IDs already asked.
        self.initial_question_count = initial_question_count
        self.technical_question_ratio = technical_question_ratio
        self.current_question_id: Optional[int] = None  # ID of the question currently being asked.
        self.interview_complete = False

        self.job_details = self.database_client.get_job_details(job_id)
        self.candidate_details = self.database_client.get_candidate_details(candidate_id)

        if not self.job_details or not self.candidate_details:
            raise ValueError("Job or Candidate details not found in the database.")

        self.context = {
            "job_title": self.job_details["title"],
            "job_description": self.job_details["description"],
            "candidate_name": self.candidate_details["name"],
            "candidate_resume": self.candidate_details["resume_text"],
        }

        self.interview_data = {  # Stores cumulative interview data for reporting.
            "questions": [],  # List of question dictionaries
            "responses": [],  # List of response dictionaries
            "evaluations": [],  # List of evaluation dictionaries
        }

    def _generate_initial_questions(self) -> None:
        """
        Generates the initial set of questions for the interview.
        """
        num_technical = int(self.initial_question_count * self.technical_question_ratio)
        num_behavioral = self.initial_question_count - num_technical

        technical_questions = self._generate_questions(num_technical, "technical")
        behavioral_questions = self._generate_questions(num_behavioral, "behavioral")

        initial_questions = technical_questions + behavioral_questions
        random.shuffle(initial_questions)  # Randomize the order

        for question in initial_questions:
            self.question_history.append(question["question_id"])

    def _generate_questions(self, num_questions: int, question_type: str) -> List[Dict]:
        """
        Helper function to generate a list of questions of a specific type.

        Args:
            num_questions (int): The number of questions to generate.
            question_type (str): The type of question ("technical" or "behavioral").

        Returns:
            List[Dict]: A list of question dictionaries.
        """
        questions = []
        for _ in range(num_questions):
            question_data = self.question_generator.generate_question(
                self.job_details["description"],
                self.candidate_details["resume_text"],
                question_type,
            )

            if not question_data:
                logging.warning(f"Failed to generate {question_type} question.")
                continue

            question_data["session_id"] = self.session_id  # Add session ID
            question_id = self.database_client.store_question(question_data)
            question_data["question_id"] = question_id  # Update with the stored ID
            questions.append(question_data)
        return questions

    def start_interview(self) -> Dict:
        """
        Starts the interview session by generating initial questions.

        Returns:
            Dict: The first question to ask the candidate.
        """
        self._generate_initial_questions()
        if not self.question_history:
            logging.error("No questions generated for the interview.")
            self.interview_complete = True
            return {"message": "No questions available. Interview cannot start."}

        self.current_question_id = self.question_history.pop(0)  # Get the first question.
        question = self.database_client.get_question(self.current_question_id)
        self.interview_data["questions"].append(question) # Add to the interview data

        return {"question": question["question_text"]}

    def get_next_question(self) -> Dict:
        """
        Gets the next question to ask the candidate.

        Returns:
            Dict: The next question or a message if the interview is complete.
        """
        if self.interview_complete:
            return {"message": "Interview is complete."}

        if not self.question_history:
            self.interview_complete = True  # No more pre-generated questions.
            return {"message": "Interview is complete. No more questions available."}

        self.current_question_id = self.question_history.pop(0)
        question = self.database_client.get_question(self.current_question_id)

        if not question:
            logging.error(f"Question with ID {self.current_question_id} not found.")
            return {"message": "Error: Question not found."}

        self.interview_data["questions"].append(question)
        return {"question": question["question_text"]}

    def submit_response(self, question: str, response: str) -> Dict:
        """
        Submits the candidate's response to the current question, evaluates the response,
        and generates a follow-up question if needed.

        Args:
            question (str): The question being answered.
            response (str): The candidate's response.

        Returns:
            Dict: A dictionary containing the next question (if any) and the evaluation of the response.
        """
        if self.interview_complete:
            return {"message": "Interview is complete."}

        if not self.current_question_id:
            logging.error("No current question ID set.")
            return {"message": "Error: No current question."}

        current_question = self.database_client.get_question(self.current_question_id)

        if not current_question or current_question["question_text"] != question:
            logging.error("Invalid question submitted.")
            return {"message": "Error: Invalid question."}

        # Store the response in the database
        response_data = {
            "session_id": self.session_id,
            "question_id": self.current_question_id,
            "response_text": response,
        }
        response_id = self.database_client.store_response(response_data)
        response_data["response_id"] = response_id # Add the response_id

        # Evaluate the response
        evaluation = self.evaluation_service.evaluate_response(
            question=question, response=response, job_description=self.job_details["description"], candidate_resume=self.candidate_details["resume_text"]
        )

        if not evaluation:
            logging.warning("Failed to evaluate the response.")
            evaluation = {}  # Provide an empty dict to avoid errors

        # Store evaluation in database
        evaluation_data = {
            "session_id": self.session_id,
            "question_id": self.current_question_id,
            "category": "overall",  # You might want to break this down into categories.
            "score": evaluation.get("overall_score", 0.0), # Default to 0 if missing
            "justification": evaluation.get("justification", "N/A"),
        }
        evaluation_id = self.database_client.store_evaluation(evaluation_data)
        evaluation_data["evaluation_id"] = evaluation_id # Add the evaluation id

        self.interview_data["responses"].append(response_data)
        self.interview_data["evaluations"].append(evaluation_data)

        # Determine next steps (follow-up or next question)
        if evaluation.get("follow_up_required", False):
            follow_up_question_data = self.question_generator.generate_question(
                self.job_details["description"],
                self.candidate_details["resume_text"],
                "follow_up",
                context=response,  # Pass the response as context for follow-up.
            )

            if follow_up_question_data:
                follow_up_question_data["session_id"] = self.session_id
                follow_up_question_id = self.database_client.store_question(follow_up_question_data)
                follow_up_question_data["question_id"] = follow_up_question_id

                self.question_history.insert(0, follow_up_question_id)  # Add to the front
                next_question_data = {"question": follow_up_question_data["question_text"]}
            else:
                next_question_data = self.get_next_question()  # Fallback to the next question if follow-up generation fails

        else:
            next_question_data = self.get_next_question()

        return {"evaluation": evaluation, **next_question_data}

    def get_interview_data(self) -> Dict:
        """
        Returns all interview data collected during the session.

        Returns:
            Dict: A dictionary containing all questions, responses, and evaluations.
        """
        return self.interview_data

    def end_interview(self) -> None:
        """
        Marks the interview as complete.
        """
        self.interview_complete = True


class DummyQuestionGenerator:  # For testing
    """
    A dummy question generator for testing purposes.
    """

    def generate_question(self, job_description: str, resume_text: str, question_type: str, context: str = None) -> Dict:
        """
        Generates a dummy question based on the question type.

        Args:
            job_description (str): The job description.
            resume_text (str): The resume text.
            question_type (str): The type of question ("technical", "behavioral", or "follow_up").
            context (str, optional): The context for generating follow-up questions. Defaults to None.

        Returns:
            Dict: A dictionary containing the question details.
        """
        if question_type == "technical":
            question = "Explain the concept of polymorphism."
        elif question_type == "behavioral":
            question = "Tell me about a time you failed."
        elif question_type == "follow_up":
            question = f"Follow-up: Elaborate on '{context[:20]}...'"  # Shorten the context for brevity.
        else:
            return None

        return {
            "question_text": question,
            "question_type": question_type,
            "expected_answer": "Some expected answer",
            "topic": "General",
            "difficulty": "medium",
        }


class DummyEvaluationService:  # For testing
    """
    A dummy evaluation service for testing purposes.
    """

    def evaluate_response(self, question: str, response: str, job_description: str, candidate_resume: str) -> Dict:
        """
        Evaluates a dummy response and returns a score.

        Args:
            question (str): The question being answered.
            response (str): The candidate's response.
            job_description (str): The job description.
            candidate_resume (str): The candidate's resume.

        Returns:
            Dict: A dictionary containing the evaluation results.
        """
        # Simulate follow-up requirement (50% chance)
        follow_up_required = random.random() < 0.5

        return {
            "relevance": 0.8,
            "depth": 0.7,
            "confidence": 0.9,
            "technical_accuracy": 0.75,
            "overall_score": 0.8,
            "justification": "The candidate provided a good response.",
            "follow_up_required": follow_up_required,
        }


class DummyDatabaseClient:
    """
    A dummy database client for testing purposes.
    """

    def __init__(self):
        self.questions = {}
        self.responses = {}
        self.evaluations = {}
        self.question_id_counter = 1
        self.response_id_counter = 1
        self.evaluation_id_counter = 1
        self.job_details = {
            123: {
                "job_id": 123,
                "title": "Software Engineer",
                "description": "Looking for a software engineer with Python and Javascript skills.",
            }
        }
        self.candidate_details = {
            456: {
                "candidate_id": 456,
                "name": "John Doe",
                "resume_text": "Experienced in Python and Javascript.",
            }
        }

    def store_question(self, question_data: Dict) -> int:
        """Stores a question in the dummy database."""
        question_id = self.question_id_counter
        self.questions[question_id] = question_data
        self.question_id_counter += 1
        logging.info(f"Stored question with ID {question_id}: {question_data['question_text']}")
        return question_id

    def store_response(self, response_data: Dict) -> int:
        """Stores a response in the dummy database."""
        response_id = self.response_id_counter
        self.responses[response_id] = response_data
        self.response_id_counter += 1
        logging.info(f"Stored response with ID {response_id}: {response_data['response_text']}")
        return response_id

    def store_evaluation(self, evaluation_data: Dict) -> int:
        """Stores evaluation data in the dummy database."""
        evaluation_id = self.evaluation_id_counter
        self.evaluations[evaluation_id] = evaluation_data
        self.evaluation_id_counter += 1
        logging.info(f"Stored evaluation with ID {evaluation_id}: {evaluation_data}")
        return evaluation_id

    def get_question(self, question_id: int) -> Optional[Dict]:
        """Retrieves a question from the dummy database."""
        return self.questions.get(question_id)

    def get_job_details(self, job_id: int) -> Optional[Dict]:
        """Retrieves job details from the dummy database."""
        return self.job_details.get(job_id)

    def get_candidate_details(self, candidate_id: int) -> Optional[Dict]:
        """Retrieves candidate details from the dummy database."""
        return self.candidate_details.get(candidate_id)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Example Usage:
    job_id = 123
    candidate_id = 456
    session_id = 789

    # Initialize the dummy services
    question_generator = DummyQuestionGenerator()
    evaluation_service = DummyEvaluationService()
    database_client = DummyDatabaseClient()

    # Initialize the InterviewOrchestrator
    orchestrator = InterviewOrchestrator(
        job_id=job_id,
        candidate_id=candidate_id,
        session_id=session_id,
        question_generator=question_generator,
        evaluation_service=evaluation_service,
        database_client=database_client,
    )

    # Start the interview
    first_question = orchestrator.start_interview()
    print(f"First Question: {first_question}")

    # Submit a response and get the next question
    if "question" in first_question:
        response = "This is a sample response."
        next_step = orchestrator.submit_response(first_question["question"], response)
        print(f"Next Step: {next_step}")

        # Continue the interview loop (simplified)
        if "question" in next_step:
            response2 = "This is another sample response."
            next_step2 = orchestrator.submit_response(next_step["question"], response2)
            print(f"Next Step 2: {next_step2}")

    # Get all interview data
    interview_data = orchestrator.get_interview_data()
    print(f"Interview Data: {json.dumps(interview_data, indent=2)}")

    # End the interview
    orchestrator.end_interview()
